questions.answerStructureHint: Dependencies, lifecycle replacement, stale closure.

expectedPoints.label: Includes every reactive input
expectedPoints.description: The shipment id and other reactive values used by the subscription belong in the dependency list.

expectedPoints.label: Cleans up the previous resource
expectedPoints.description: Cleanup unsubscribes before the effect is replaced or the component unmounts.

expectedPoints.label: Explains stale closures
expectedPoints.description: Missing dependencies can make callbacks keep old state or props even while the UI changed.

followUps.prompt: Why can suppressing the exhaustive-deps warning create intermittent bugs?

hu.title: Effect cleanup és dependency-k
hu.prompt: Magyarázd el a dependency array és cleanup function szerepét egy live shipment update-re subscribe-oló effectnél.
hu.answerStructureHint: Dependency-k, lifecycle replacement, stale closure.

effect_dependencies.label: Minden reactive inputot megad
effect_dependencies.description: A shipment id és a subscription által használt egyéb reactive érték a dependency listába kerül.

effect_cleanup.label: Eltakarítja a korábbi resource-t
effect_cleanup.description: Cleanup unsubscribe-ol az effect lecserélése vagy component unmount előtt.

effect_stale.label: Elmagyarázza a stale closure-t
effect_stale.description: Hiányzó dependency miatt a callback régi state-et vagy propsot tarthat meg.

followUps.redspher_react_effect_cleanup_followup: Miért okozhat időszakos bugot az exhaustive-deps warning elnyomása?

questions.title: Diagnosing a slow React interaction
questions.prompt: A shipment table becomes slow while typing into a filter. Describe how you would diagnose the bottleneck before changing the code.
questions.answerStructureHint: Reproduce, profile, isolate.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a measurable interaction
expectedPoints.description: Reproduces with representative data and observes input latency, render duration and network behavior.

expectedPoints.label: Uses profiling evidence
expectedPoints.description: Uses React Profiler and browser performance tools to find expensive components, JavaScript or layout.

expectedPoints.label: Separates frontend and backend causes
expectedPoints.description: Checks response size and timing so a network or data-volume problem is not misdiagnosed as rendering.

followUps.prompt: Which metric would tell you that the change improved the user experience?

hu.title: Lassú React interaction diagnosztikája
hu.prompt: A shipment table filterbe gépelés közben lelassul. Mutasd be, hogyan diagnosztizálnád a bottlenecket kódmódosítás előtt.
hu.answerStructureHint: Reprodukció, profiling, izolálás.

perf_reproduce.label: Mérhető interactiont definiál
perf_reproduce.description: Reprezentatív adattal reprodukál, és input latencyt, render durationt, network viselkedést figyel.

perf_profile.label: Profiling evidence-et használ
perf_profile.description: React Profilerrel és browser performance toolokkal keres drága componentet, JavaScriptet vagy layoutot.

perf_isolate.label: Elválasztja a frontend és backend okot
perf_isolate.description: Response size-t és timingot ellenőriz, hogy network vagy data-volume problémát ne render hibának nézzen.

followUps.redspher_react_perf_measure_followup: Mely metric mutatná meg, hogy a változtatás javította a user experience-et?

questions.title: Choosing a React performance fix
questions.prompt: Profiling shows that a large table rerenders every row on each keystroke. What options would you consider, and how would you choose?
questions.answerStructureHint: Reduce work, stabilize boundaries, verify.

expectedPoints.label: Reduces rendered work
expectedPoints.description: Considers virtualization, pagination or deferring the expensive result update.

expectedPoints.label: Stabilizes only useful boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Uses memoization or stable props only where profiling shows repeated expensive work.

expectedPoints.label: Measures after the change
expectedPoints.description: Repeats the same profile and checks responsiveness, memory and complexity trade-offs.

followUps.prompt: Why might React.memo fail to help even when a child rerenders often?

hu.title: React performance fix választása
hu.prompt: A profiling szerint egy nagy table minden billentyűre újrarendereli az összes row-t. Milyen opciókat mérlegelnél, és hogyan választanál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Munka csökkentése, boundary stabilizálás, ellenőrzés.

perf_reduce.label: Csökkenti a renderelt munkát
perf_reduce.description: Virtualizationt, paginationt vagy a drága result update deferálását mérlegeli.

perf_boundaries.label: Csak hasznos boundaryt stabilizál
perf_boundaries.description: Memoizationt vagy stable propsot csak mért, ismétlődő drága munkánál használ.

perf_verify.label: Mérés után ellenőriz
perf_verify.description: Megismétli ugyanazt a profilt, és response, memory illetve complexity trade-offot vizsgál.

followUps.redspher_react_perf_fix_followup: Miért nem segít feltétlen a React.memo akkor sem, ha egy child gyakran rerenderel?

questions.title: Memoization trade-offs
questions.prompt: When are useMemo, useCallback and React.memo useful, and when do they make a codebase worse?
questions.answerStructureHint: Purpose, prerequisites, cost.

expectedPoints.label: Explains what memoization reuses
expectedPoints.description: It reuses a calculation, function identity or component result while relevant inputs stay equal.

expectedPoints.label: Names evidence-based cases
expectedPoints.description: It helps with expensive calculations or memoized children sensitive to reference identity.

expectedPoints.label: Recognizes maintenance and runtime cost
expectedPoints.description: Memoization adds comparisons, retained values and dependency complexity, so cheap work may become less clear without meaningful gain.

followUps.prompt: What would you measure before and after adding memoization?

hu.title: Memoization trade-offok
hu.prompt: Mikor hasznos a useMemo, useCallback és React.memo, és mikor rontják a codebase-t?
hu.answerStructureHint: Cél, előfeltétel, költség.

memo_purpose.label: Elmagyarázza, mit használ újra
memo_purpose.description: Calculationt, function identityt vagy component resultot használ újra változatlan input mellett.

memo_useful.label: Evidence-based esetet mond
memo_useful.description: Drága calculationnél vagy reference identityre érzékeny memoized childnál hasznos.

memo_cost.label: Felismeri a költséget
memo_cost.description: Comparisont, retained valuet és dependency complexityt ad, ezért olcsó munkánál csak rontja az érthetőséget.

followUps.redspher_react_perf_memo_followup: Mit mérnél memoization hozzáadása előtt és után?

questions.title: Node.js event loop under load
questions.prompt: Explain why Node.js can handle many concurrent requests and give an example of work that would still block the service.
questions.answerStructureHint: I/O waiting, JavaScript thread, blocking example.

expectedPoints.label: Explains non-blocking I/O
expectedPoints.description: The runtime can wait for network, file or database I/O while scheduling other ready work.

expectedPoints.label: Explains JavaScript execution
expectedPoints.description: JavaScript callbacks normally run one at a time on the main event-loop thread.

expectedPoints.label: Gives a realistic blocking case
expectedPoints.description: Large synchronous CPU work, parsing or loops can delay every request.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect event-loop blocking in production?

hu.title: Node.js event loop terhelés alatt
hu.prompt: Magyarázd el, miért tud a Node.js sok concurrent requestet kezelni, és mondj példát olyan munkára, amely mégis blokkolná a service-t.
hu.answerStructureHint: I/O várakozás, JavaScript szál, blokkoló példa.

node_io.label: Elmagyarázza a non-blocking I/O-t
node_io.description: A runtime network, file vagy database I/O-ra várhat, miközben más futtatható munkát ütemez.

node_thread.label: Elmagyarázza a JavaScript executiont
node_thread.description: A JavaScript callbackek normálisan egyenként futnak a fő event-loop szálon.

node_block.label: Reális blokkoló példát ad
node_block.description: Nagy synchronous CPU work, parsing vagy loop minden requestet késleltethet.

followUps.redspher_node_event_loop_followup: Hogyan észlelnéd productionben az event-loop blockingot?

questions.title: Bounding asynchronous concurrency
questions.prompt: You must enrich 20,000 shipments by calling a carrier API. Why is one Promise.all risky, and how would you design the processing?
questions.answerStructureHint: Overload risk, bounded workers, failure handling.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies the concurrency burst
expectedPoints.description: Starting all calls at once can exhaust sockets, memory, quotas and the carrier service.

expectedPoints.label: Uses bounded concurrency
expectedPoints.description: Processes with a concurrency limit, worker pool or queue that matches dependency capacity.

expectedPoints.label: Plans retries and progress
expectedPoints.description: Uses timeouts, selective retry, idempotency and durable progress so partial failures can resume.

followUps.prompt: How would you choose the initial concurrency limit?

hu.title: Aszinkron concurrency korlátozása
hu.prompt: Húszezer shipmentet kell enrich-elni carrier API hívásokkal. Miért kockázatos egyetlen Promise.all, és hogyan terveznéd a feldolgozást?
hu.answerStructureHint: Overload risk, bounded worker, failure handling.

node_burst.label: Felismeri a concurrency burstöt
node_burst.description: Minden hívás egyszerre indítva socketet, memoryt, quotát és carrier service-t terhel túl.

node_bound.label: Bounded concurrencyt használ
node_bound.description: Concurrency limittel, worker poollal vagy dependency capacityhez igazított queue-val dolgozik.

node_failures.label: Megtervezi a failure kezelést
node_failures.description: Timeoutot, selective retryt, idempotencyt és durable progresst használ partial failure utáni folytatáshoz.

followUps.redspher_node_concurrency_followup: Hogyan választanád ki a kezdeti concurrency limitet?

questions.title: Handling CPU-heavy work in Node.js
questions.prompt: A route-optimization calculation takes several seconds of CPU time. What implementation options would you consider in a Node.js system?
questions.answerStructureHint: Protect event loop, execution options, operational choice.

expectedPoints.label: Protects the event loop
expectedPoints.description: The calculation should not run synchronously on the request thread because it delays unrelated traffic.

expectedPoints.label: Compares suitable execution options
expectedPoints.description: Considers worker threads, a separate service, a queue worker or a specialized runtime depending on duration and scale.

expectedPoints.label: Designs asynchronous user experience when needed
expectedPoints.description: For long work, returns a job id and exposes progress or completion instead of holding an HTTP request.

followUps.prompt: What would make you choose worker threads over a separate service?

hu.title: CPU-heavy munka kezelése Node.js-ben
hu.prompt: Egy route-optimization számítás több másodperc CPU-időt használ. Milyen implementációs opciókat mérlegelnél Node.js rendszerben?
hu.answerStructureHint: Event loop védelme, execution opciók, operational döntés.

node_cpu_risk.label: Védi az event loopot
node_cpu_risk.description: A számítás ne fusson synchronously a request szálon, mert unrelated trafficet is késleltet.

node_cpu_options.label: Megfelelő execution opciókat hasonlít
node_cpu_options.description: Worker threadet, separate service-t, queue workert vagy specialized runtime-ot mérlegel duration és scale alapján.

node_cpu_contract.label: Szükség esetén aszinkron UX-et tervez
node_cpu_contract.description: Hosszú munkánál job id-t ad, és progress vagy completion elérést biztosít a HTTP request nyitva tartása helyett.

followUps.redspher_node_cpu_followup: Mitől választanál worker threadet külön service helyett?

questions.title: Designing a shipment REST API
questions.prompt: Design the main endpoints for reading a shipment, updating selected fields and requesting a repricing operation. Explain your HTTP choices.
questions.answerStructureHint: Resources, methods, command semantics.

expectedPoints.label: Models stable resources
expectedPoints.description: Uses clear shipment identifiers and resource-oriented paths for retrieval and ordinary updates.

expectedPoints.label: Uses method semantics deliberately
expectedPoints.description: Uses GET safely, PATCH for partial changes, and a clear command or subresource for repricing.

expectedPoints.label: Defines outcomes and concurrency behavior
expectedPoints.description: Covers validation, not found, authorization, accepted async work and conflicting updates.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent one user from overwriting a shipment changed by another user?

hu.title: Shipment REST API tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezd meg a fő endpointokat shipment lekérésére, kiválasztott mezők módosítására és repricing kérésére. Indokold a HTTP döntéseidet.
hu.answerStructureHint: Resource-ok, methodok, command szemantika.

rest_resource.label: Stabil resource-okat modellez
rest_resource.description: Tiszta shipment identifierrel és resource-oriented pathszal tervez retrievalt és normál update-et.

rest_methods.label: Tudatos HTTP method szemantikát használ
rest_methods.description: GET-et safe lekérésre, PATCH-et partial change-re, repricinghoz pedig tiszta commandot vagy subresource-t használ.

rest_outcomes.label: Definiálja az outcome-okat
rest_outcomes.description: Kitér validationre, not foundra, authorizationre, accepted async workre és conflicting update-re.

followUps.redspher_rest_resource_followup: Hogyan akadályoznád meg, hogy egy user felülírja egy másik user közben történt módosítását?

questions.title: Recognizing breaking API changes
questions.prompt: Which API changes are breaking for existing clients? Discuss removing a field, adding an optional field and adding a new enum value.
questions.answerStructureHint: Consumer perspective, examples, enum nuance.

expectedPoints.label: Defines breaking from the consumer view
expectedPoints.description: A change is breaking when a previously valid client can no longer parse, call or interpret the contract correctly.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies common field changes
expectedPoints.description: Removing or changing meaning is usually breaking; a truly optional additive field is usually compatible.

expectedPoints.label: Recognizes enum expansion risk
expectedPoints.description: A new enum value can break exhaustive or closed-enum clients even though the schema change looks additive.

followUps.prompt: How could you design clients to tolerate future enum values?

hu.title: Breaking API change felismerése
hu.prompt: Mely API változások breakingek meglévő kliensek számára? Beszélj mező eltávolításáról, optional mező hozzáadásáról és új enum értékről.
hu.answerStructureHint: Consumer nézőpont, példák, enum nuance.

rest_consumer.label: Consumer nézőpontból definiál
rest_consumer.description: Breaking az a change, amelynél korábban valid kliens már nem tudja helyesen parse-olni, hívni vagy értelmezni a contractot.

rest_field_changes.label: Osztályozza a field change-eket
rest_field_changes.description: Mező eltávolítása vagy jelentésváltása általában breaking; valóban optional additív mező többnyire kompatibilis.

rest_enum.label: Felismeri az enum expansion risket
rest_enum.description: Új enum value eltörhet exhaustive vagy closed-enum klienst, bár a schema change additívnak látszik.

followUps.redspher_rest_breaking_followup: Hogyan tervezhetnél klienst úgy, hogy tolerálja a jövőbeli enum értékeket?

questions.title: Designing an API error contract
questions.prompt: What should a consistent REST API error response contain, and how should it differ for validation, authentication and unexpected server failures?
questions.answerStructureHint: Stable shape, correct status, safe diagnostics.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a stable machine-readable shape
expectedPoints.description: Includes a stable code, human-safe message, optional field details and correlation id.

expectedPoints.label: Uses meaningful HTTP status codes
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes invalid input, missing or invalid identity, forbidden action and unexpected server failure.

expectedPoints.label: Separates client and internal diagnostics
expectedPoints.description: Clients get stable safe information; structured logs retain stack and dependency context using the correlation id.

followUps.prompt: Should clients retry a 500 response automatically? What else must they know?

hu.title: API error contract tervezése
hu.prompt: Mit tartalmazzon egy konzisztens REST API error response, és hogyan térjen el validation, authentication és unexpected server failure esetén?
hu.answerStructureHint: Stabil forma, helyes status, biztonságos diagnosztika.

rest_error_shape.label: Stabil machine-readable formát definiál
rest_error_shape.description: Stabil code-ot, biztonságos message-et, opcionális field detailt és correlation id-t ad.

rest_error_status.label: Jelentésteli HTTP status code-okat használ
rest_error_status.description: Elválasztja az invalid inputot, hiányzó vagy invalid identityt, forbidden actiont és unexpected server failure-t.

rest_error_safety.label: Elválasztja a client és belső diagnosztikát
rest_error_safety.description: A kliens stabil, safe információt kap; a log stack és dependency contextet tart meg correlation id-val.

followUps.redspher_rest_errors_followup: Automatikusan retryoljon a kliens 500 response-ra? Mit kell még tudnia?

questions.title: Deciding whether to retry
questions.prompt: A carrier booking request times out. How do you decide whether and how to retry it safely?
questions.answerStructureHint: Failure type, idempotency, bounded retry.

expectedPoints.label: Recognizes the ambiguous outcome
expectedPoints.description: A timeout does not prove the carrier failed; the booking may already exist.

expectedPoints.label: Requires idempotency or reconciliation
expectedPoints.description: Uses an idempotency key, provider operation id or status lookup before repeating a side effect.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a bounded policy
expectedPoints.description: Retries only transient failures with timeout, backoff, jitter and a limit, then moves to recovery.

followUps.prompt: What would you show the user while the booking outcome is unknown?

hu.title: Retry döntés
hu.prompt: Egy carrier booking request timeoutol. Hogyan döntöd el, hogy retryolható-e és hogyan lehet biztonságosan retryolni?
hu.answerStructureHint: Failure típus, idempotency, bounded retry.

retry_ambiguity.label: Felismeri az ambiguous outcome-ot
retry_ambiguity.description: A timeout nem bizonyítja a carrier failure-t; a booking már létrejöhetett.

retry_idempotency.label: Idempotencyt vagy reconciliationt követel
retry_idempotency.description: Idempotency key-t, provider operation id-t vagy status lookupot használ side effect ismétlése előtt.

retry_policy.label: Bounded policyt használ
retry_policy.description: Csak transient failure-t retryol timeouttal, backoffal, jitterrel és limittel, majd recoveryre vált.

followUps.redspher_resilience_retry_followup: Mit mutatnál a usernek, amíg a booking outcome ismeretlen?

questions.title: Using a circuit breaker
questions.prompt: What problem does a circuit breaker solve, and where would you place it when a Node.js service depends on an unstable pricing API?
questions.answerStructureHint: Purpose, states, fallback and scope.

expectedPoints.label: Explains failure containment
expectedPoints.description: It stops repeated calls to a failing dependency so resources and latency are protected.

expectedPoints.label: Explains recovery probing
expectedPoints.description: After opening, it waits and later allows limited probe calls before closing.

expectedPoints.label: Places it at the dependency boundary
expectedPoints.description: Wraps the pricing client with metrics and a deliberate fallback or error path, not the whole application blindly.

followUps.prompt: What could go wrong if every service instance maintains an independent circuit state?

hu.title: Circuit breaker használata
hu.prompt: Milyen problémát old meg a circuit breaker, és hová helyeznéd, ha egy Node.js service instabil pricing API-tól függ?
hu.answerStructureHint: Cél, state-ek, fallback és scope.

circuit_purpose.label: Elmagyarázza a failure containmentet
circuit_purpose.description: Leállítja a repeated callokat hibázó dependency felé, így védi a resource-okat és latencyt.

circuit_states.label: Elmagyarázza a recovery probingot
circuit_states.description: Open után vár, majd limitált probe callt enged, és siker esetén zár.

circuit_scope.label: Dependency boundaryre helyezi
circuit_scope.description: A pricing clientet wrapeli metric-kel és tudatos fallback vagy error pathszal, nem vakon a teljes alkalmazást.

followUps.redspher_resilience_circuit_followup: Mi lehet a gond, ha minden service instance külön circuit state-et tart?

questions.title: Handling errors across application layers
questions.prompt: How would errors flow from a database or external client through domain and API layers without being swallowed or leaking implementation details?
questions.answerStructureHint: Classification, context, translation.

expectedPoints.label: Classifies technical and domain failures
expectedPoints.description: Separates expected domain outcomes, invalid input, missing data and unexpected infrastructure failures.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves the original cause and context
expectedPoints.description: Adds operation and identifiers while retaining the cause for logs and tracing.

expectedPoints.label: Translates only at boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Maps internal failures to stable API errors at the HTTP boundary and avoids leaking database or stack details.

followUps.prompt: When is it appropriate to catch an error and continue?

hu.title: Hibakezelés application layereken át
hu.prompt: Hogyan áramoljanak a hibák database vagy external client rétegből domain és API layeren át anélkül, hogy lenyelnék őket vagy implementation detail szivárogna ki?
hu.answerStructureHint: Classification, context, translation.

error_classify.label: Osztályozza a technical és domain failure-t
error_classify.description: Elválasztja az expected domain outcome-ot, invalid inputot, missing datát és unexpected infrastructure failure-t.

error_context.label: Megőrzi az eredeti cause-t és contextet
error_context.description: Operationt és identifiert ad hozzá, miközben loghoz és tracinghez megtartja a cause-t.

error_translate.label: Csak boundaryn fordít
error_translate.description: A HTTP boundaryn mapeli belső failure-t stabil API errorra, database vagy stack detail kiszivárgása nélkül.

followUps.redspher_resilience_error_layers_followu: Mikor helyes catch-elni egy errort és folytatni a működést?

questions.title: Designing an end-to-end logistics feature
questions.prompt: Design an end-to-end feature that lets an operations user manually reprice a shipment. Cover frontend, backend, data, security and rollout.
questions.answerStructureHint: Journey and contract, integrity and authorization, operation.

expectedPoints.label: Traces one complete user flow
expectedPoints.description: Defines selection, current price display, edit or command, confirmation and clear success or failure states.

expectedPoints.label: Protects business and data integrity
expectedPoints.description: Uses validation, authorization, optimistic concurrency or versioning, and audit history.

expectedPoints.label: Includes tests, telemetry and rollout
expectedPoints.description: Defines contract and end-to-end tests, key metrics, feature flag rollout and rollback.

followUps.prompt: How would the design change if repricing takes thirty seconds?

hu.title: End-to-end logistics feature tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezz end-to-end feature-t, amelyben egy operations user manuálisan repricingol shipmentet. Térj ki frontend, backend, data, security és rollout kérdésekre.
hu.answerStructureHint: Journey és contract, integrity és authorization, üzemeltetés.

e2e_flow.label: Végigkövet egy teljes user flow-t
e2e_flow.description: Definiál selectiont, current price megjelenítést, editet vagy commandot, confirmationt és tiszta success/failure state-et.

e2e_integrity.label: Védi a business és data integrityt
e2e_integrity.description: Validationt, authorizationt, optimistic concurrencyt vagy versioninget és audit historyt használ.

e2e_rollout.label: Tesztet, telemetryt és rolloutot is tervez
e2e_rollout.description: Contract és end-to-end testet, kulcs metricet, feature flag rolloutot és rollbacket definiál.

followUps.redspher_e2e_feature_followup: Hogyan változna a design, ha a repricing harminc másodpercig tartana?

questions.title: Coordinating frontend and backend delivery
questions.prompt: How would you split and coordinate work when one developer owns both sides of a new feature but frontend and backend may deploy independently?
questions.answerStructureHint: Contract first, compatible sequence, incremental slice.

expectedPoints.label: Defines the contract before coupling implementations
expectedPoints.description: Agrees schemas, error behavior and examples, ideally with generated clients or contract tests.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a backward-compatible deployment sequence
expectedPoints.description: Deploys additive backend support first, then the frontend, and removes old behavior only after consumers migrate.

expectedPoints.label: Delivers a thin vertical slice
expectedPoints.description: Builds a small usable path with tests and telemetry before expanding secondary cases.

followUps.prompt: How would you test compatibility when two frontend versions may be active?

hu.title: Frontend és backend delivery koordinálása
hu.prompt: Hogyan bontanád és koordinálnád a munkát, ha egy developer mindkét oldalt viszi, de a frontend és backend külön deployolhat?
hu.answerStructureHint: Contract first, kompatibilis sorrend, incremental slice.

e2e_contract.label: Implementation előtt contractot definiál
e2e_contract.description: Schemát, error behaviort és példákat rögzít, lehetőleg generated clienttel vagy contract testtel.

e2e_sequence.label: Backward-compatible deploy sorrendet használ
e2e_sequence.description: Additív backend support kerül ki először, utána frontend, régi behavior csak consumer migration után tűnik el.

e2e_slice.label: Thin vertical slice-t szállít
e2e_slice.description: Kis használható pathot épít testtel és telemetryvel a secondary case-ek előtt.

followUps.redspher_e2e_contract_followup: Hogyan tesztelnéd a compatibilityt, ha két frontend verzió is aktív lehet?

questions.title: Defining done for a production feature
questions.prompt: What does done mean for a business-critical full-stack feature beyond the code being merged?
questions.answerStructureHint: User behavior, operational readiness, ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Validates acceptance behavior
expectedPoints.description: Acceptance criteria and important failure cases pass with representative data and permissions.

expectedPoints.label: Includes production readiness
expectedPoints.description: Deployment, migration, monitoring, alerting, security and rollback are ready.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies support and measurement
expectedPoints.description: The team knows who owns incidents and which business and technical metrics show success.

followUps.prompt: Which parts of this definition would you automate in CI/CD?

hu.title: Production feature definition of done
hu.prompt: Mit jelent a done egy business-critical full-stack featurenél azon túl, hogy a kód merge-elve van?
hu.answerStructureHint: User behavior, operational readiness, ownership.

done_behavior.label: Validálja az acceptance behaviort
done_behavior.description: Acceptance criteria és fontos failure case-ek reprezentatív adattal és permissionnel passzolnak.

done_operate.label: Tartalmaz production readinesst
done_operate.description: Deployment, migration, monitoring, alerting, security és rollback készen áll.

done_ownership.label: Tisztázza az ownershipöt
done_ownership.description: A csapat tudja, ki kezeli az incidentet, és mely business illetve technical metric mutat successt.

followUps.redspher_e2e_definition_done_followup: A definition mely részeit automatizálnád CI/CD-ben?

questions.title: Choosing AWS compute for a Node.js API
questions.prompt: Compare Lambda, a managed container service and Kubernetes for a new Node.js API with uneven traffic and a small team.
questions.answerStructureHint: Workload fit, operations, decision.

expectedPoints.label: Explains Lambda fit and limits
expectedPoints.description: Lambda fits event-driven or bursty short work but has runtime, cold-start and execution constraints.

expectedPoints.label: Explains managed container fit
expectedPoints.description: A managed container platform suits long-running HTTP services with more runtime control and moderate operational burden.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids unjustified Kubernetes
expectedPoints.description: Kubernetes is justified by broader platform needs and expertise, not merely because the API may grow.

followUps.prompt: Which measurements after launch could cause you to revisit the compute choice?

hu.title: AWS compute választása Node.js API-hoz
hu.prompt: Hasonlítsd össze a Lambdát, managed container service-t és Kubernetes-t egy ingadozó trafficű új Node.js API-hoz, kis csapattal.
hu.answerStructureHint: Workload fit, operations, döntés.

aws_lambda.label: Elmagyarázza a Lambda fitet és limiteket
aws_lambda.description: Lambda bursty vagy event-driven rövid munkára jó, de runtime, cold-start és execution constraintje van.

aws_container.label: Elmagyarázza a managed container fitet
aws_container.description: Managed container platform long-running HTTP service-hez jó, több runtime controllal és mérsékelt operational burdennel.

aws_k8s.label: Kerüli az indokolatlan Kubernetest
aws_k8s.description: Kubernetes szélesebb platform need és expertise miatt indokolt, nem pusztán várható növekedés miatt.

followUps.redspher_aws_choose_compute_followup: Mely launch utáni mérések miatt gondolnád újra a compute választást?

questions.title: Selecting AWS services for an asynchronous workflow
questions.prompt: A pricing request may take minutes and must survive restarts. Sketch an AWS-based workflow and explain the role of each service category.
questions.answerStructureHint: Durable intake, processing and state, user result.

expectedPoints.label: Creates durable asynchronous intake
expectedPoints.description: The API validates, creates an operation id and places work in a durable queue or workflow.

expectedPoints.label: Uses controlled workers and durable state
expectedPoints.description: Workers process with bounded concurrency, retries and a durable status store or orchestrator.

expectedPoints.label: Exposes completion safely
expectedPoints.description: The client polls a status resource, receives an event or notification, and can retrieve the result idempotently.

followUps.prompt: Where would you place a dead-letter queue, and what should happen to those messages?

hu.title: AWS service-ek választása aszinkron workflow-hoz
hu.prompt: Egy pricing request percekig tarthat és túl kell élnie a restartot. Vázolj AWS-alapú workflow-t, és indokold az egyes service kategóriák szerepét.
hu.answerStructureHint: Durable intake, processing és state, user result.

aws_intake.label: Durable aszinkron intake-et készít
aws_intake.description: Az API validál, operation id-t hoz létre, és durable queue-ba vagy workflow-ba teszi a munkát.

aws_process.label: Controlled workert és durable state-et használ
aws_process.description: Worker bounded concurrencyvel és retryjal dolgozik, durable status store vagy orchestrator mellett.

aws_result.label: Biztonságosan adja vissza a completiont
aws_result.description: A kliens status resource-t pollol, eventet vagy notificationt kap, és idempotensen lekéri az eredményt.

followUps.redspher_aws_event_workflow_followup: Hová tennél dead-letter queue-t, és mi történjen az oda került message-ekkel?

questions.title: Balancing AWS cost and simplicity
questions.prompt: How would you estimate and control AWS cost for a prototype that may become a core logistics application?
questions.answerStructureHint: Cost drivers, staged design, controls.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies workload cost drivers
expectedPoints.description: Estimates requests, compute duration, storage, database access, logs and data transfer from explicit assumptions.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps the prototype evolvable but simple
expectedPoints.description: Starts with managed services and clean boundaries instead of prebuilding maximum scale.

expectedPoints.label: Adds budgets and observability
expectedPoints.description: Uses tags, budgets, alerts, dashboards and periodic unit-cost review before usage surprises grow.

followUps.prompt: What is an example of a cheap prototype choice that becomes expensive at scale?

hu.title: AWS cost és egyszerűség egyensúlya
hu.prompt: Hogyan becsülnéd és kontrollálnád az AWS costot egy prototype-nál, amely core logistics applicationné válhat?
hu.answerStructureHint: Cost driver, staged design, controlok.

aws_drivers.label: Azonosítja a workload cost drivereket
aws_drivers.description: Explicit assumptionből becsül requestet, compute durationt, storage-ot, database accesst, logot és data transfert.

aws_stage.label: A prototype egyszerű, de továbbfejleszthető
aws_stage.description: Managed service-szel és clean boundaryval indul, maximum scale előreépítése nélkül.

aws_controls.label: Budgetet és observabilityt ad
aws_controls.description: Taget, budgetet, alertet, dashboardot és rendszeres unit-cost review-t használ.

followUps.redspher_aws_cost_followup: Mi példa olyan olcsó prototype döntésre, amely scale-nél drágává válik?

questions.title: Removing single points of failure on AWS
questions.prompt: Review a Node.js service running on one virtual machine with a local database. What would you change first to improve availability, and why?
questions.answerStructureHint: Failure analysis, redundancy, state and recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Identifies failure domains and critical state
expectedPoints.description: The machine, process, disk and availability zone are explicit failure points; the database state is the highest recovery risk.

expectedPoints.label: Adds health-based redundant compute
expectedPoints.description: Runs stateless instances across failure domains behind health checks and load balancing.

expectedPoints.label: Moves state to a managed durable service
expectedPoints.description: Uses a managed database with backups, multi-zone options and a tested restore or failover plan.

followUps.prompt: Would you make every component multi-region? Why or why not?

hu.title: Single point of failure megszüntetése AWS-en
hu.prompt: Nézz át egy Node.js service-t, amely egy virtual machine-en fut local database-szel. Mit változtatnál először az availability javításához, és miért?
hu.answerStructureHint: Failure analysis, redundancia, state és recovery.

aws_rel_failure.label: Azonosítja a failure domaineket és critical state-et
aws_rel_failure.description: A machine, process, disk és availability zone explicit failure point; a database state a legnagyobb recovery risk.

aws_rel_redundancy.label: Health-based redundant compute-ot ad
aws_rel_redundancy.description: Stateless instance-eket futtat több failure domainben health check és load balancing mögött.

aws_rel_data.label: Managed durable service-be viszi a state-et
aws_rel_data.description: Managed database-t használ backup, multi-zone opció és tesztelt restore vagy failover plan mellett.

followUps.redspher_aws_reliability_followup: Minden komponenst multi-regionné tennél? Miért vagy miért nem?

questions.title: Designing least-privilege access
questions.prompt: A Node.js service needs to read one secret, consume one queue and update one database table. How would you design its AWS permissions?
questions.answerStructureHint: Workload identity, narrow policy, lifecycle.

expectedPoints.label: Uses a workload role rather than static keys
expectedPoints.description: The runtime receives temporary credentials through an attached role or task identity.

expectedPoints.label: Scopes actions and resources
expectedPoints.description: Allows only required actions on the exact secret, queue and table, with conditions where useful.

expectedPoints.label: Plans review and audit
expectedPoints.description: Uses infrastructure as code, access logging and periodic review instead of manual policy drift.

followUps.prompt: How would developers access the same resources locally without sharing production credentials?

hu.title: Least-privilege access tervezése
hu.prompt: Egy Node.js service-nek egy secretet kell olvasnia, egy queue-t fogyasztania és egy database table-t update-elnie. Hogyan terveznéd az AWS permissionjeit?
hu.answerStructureHint: Workload identity, szűk policy, lifecycle.

aws_iam_role.label: Workload role-t használ static key helyett
aws_iam_role.description: A runtime attached role vagy task identity útján temporary credentialt kap.

aws_iam_scope.label: Actionre és resource-ra szűkít
aws_iam_scope.description: Csak a szükséges actionöket engedi az adott secretre, queue-ra és table-re, indokolt conditionnel.

aws_iam_ops.label: Review-t és auditot tervez
aws_iam_ops.description: Infrastructure as code-ot, access logot és periodic review-t használ manual policy drift helyett.

followUps.redspher_aws_iam_followup: Hogyan férnének hozzá a developerek localból ugyanazokhoz a resource-okhoz production credential megosztása nélkül?

questions.title: Setting and testing recovery objectives
questions.prompt: How would you discuss RTO and RPO with business stakeholders for a core pricing or tracking application?
questions.answerStructureHint: Business impact, definitions, testable design.

expectedPoints.label: Explains the objectives plainly
expectedPoints.description: RTO is acceptable restoration time; RPO is acceptable data loss measured in time.

expectedPoints.label: Connects values to business impact
expectedPoints.description: Uses operational deadlines, manual workarounds, customer impact and cost to choose realistic targets.

expectedPoints.label: Turns targets into tested mechanisms
expectedPoints.description: Selects backup, replication and failover mechanisms and verifies them with restore exercises.

followUps.prompt: Why can a very low RPO significantly increase cost and complexity?

hu.title: Recovery objective meghatározása és tesztelése
hu.prompt: Hogyan beszélnél RTO-ról és RPO-ról business stakeholder-ekkel egy core pricing vagy tracking alkalmazásnál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Business impact, definíciók, tesztelhető design.

aws_recovery_defs.label: Egyszerűen elmagyarázza az objective-eket
aws_recovery_defs.description: Az RTO elfogadható restore idő, az RPO időben mért elfogadható adatvesztés.

aws_recovery_business.label: Business impacthez köti az értékeket
aws_recovery_business.description: Operational deadline, manual workaround, customer impact és cost alapján választ reális targetet.

aws_recovery_test.label: Tesztelt mechanizmussá alakítja a targetet
aws_recovery_test.description: Backup, replication és failover mechanizmust választ, majd restore exercise-szel ellenőrzi.

followUps.redspher_aws_recovery_followup: Miért növelheti jelentősen a costot és complexityt a nagyon alacsony RPO?

questions.title: Designing a full-stack CI/CD pipeline
questions.prompt: Sketch a CI/CD pipeline for a React frontend and Node.js backend deployed to AWS. Which checks belong at each stage?
questions.answerStructureHint: Fast feedback, artifact promotion, safe release.

expectedPoints.label: Creates fast pull-request gates
expectedPoints.description: Runs formatting or linting, type checks, focused unit tests, secret or dependency checks and production builds.

expectedPoints.label: Builds and promotes immutable artifacts
expectedPoints.description: Stores versioned frontend and backend artifacts once and promotes the exact outputs.

expectedPoints.label: Uses environment and release verification
expectedPoints.description: Runs integration or contract tests, deploys progressively, checks health metrics and can roll back.

followUps.prompt: Which tests would you avoid running on every commit, and where would they run instead?

hu.title: Full-stack CI/CD pipeline tervezése
hu.prompt: Vázolj CI/CD pipeline-t AWS-re deployolt React frontendhez és Node.js backendhez. Mely check melyik stage-be kerüljön?
hu.answerStructureHint: Gyors feedback, artifact promotion, safe release.

cicd_pr.label: Gyors pull-request gate-et készít
cicd_pr.description: Formatting vagy lint, type check, fókuszált unit test, secret vagy dependency check és production build fut.

cicd_artifact.label: Immutable artifactot buildel és promote-ol
cicd_artifact.description: Versioned frontend és backend artifactot egyszer készít, majd pontosan azt lépteti tovább.

cicd_release.label: Environment és release verificationt használ
cicd_release.description: Integration vagy contract testet futtat, progressively deployol, health metricet ellenőriz és rollbackelni tud.

followUps.redspher_cicd_pipeline_followup: Mely teszteket nem futtatnád minden commitnál, és hol futnának helyette?

questions.title: Responding to a failed production deployment
questions.prompt: A deployment completes, but error rate rises and one business workflow starts failing. What should the pipeline and team do?
questions.answerStructureHint: Detection, containment, diagnosis.

expectedPoints.label: Uses automatic release health criteria
expectedPoints.description: Metrics, logs and synthetic or smoke checks compare the new release with a known baseline.

expectedPoints.label: Stops or reverses the rollout
expectedPoints.description: Pauses promotion, routes traffic away or rolls back the immutable artifact before extended diagnosis.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves evidence and follows up
expectedPoints.description: Uses release identifiers and traces to isolate the change, then adds a regression test or safer rollout rule.

followUps.prompt: When might roll-forward be safer than rollback?

hu.title: Sikertelen production deployment kezelése
hu.prompt: A deployment befejeződik, de nő az error rate és egy business workflow hibázni kezd. Mit tegyen a pipeline és a csapat?
hu.answerStructureHint: Detection, containment, diagnózis.

cicd_detect.label: Automated release health criteriont használ
cicd_detect.description: Metric, log és synthetic vagy smoke check hasonlítja az új release-t ismert baseline-hoz.

cicd_contain.label: Megállítja vagy visszafordítja a rolloutot
cicd_contain.description: Promotiont pause-ol, trafficet elvezet vagy immutable artifactot rollbackel a hosszabb diagnózis előtt.

cicd_diagnose.label: Megőrzi az evidence-et és follow-upot végez
cicd_diagnose.description: Release id és trace alapján izolál, majd regression testet vagy biztonságosabb rollout rule-t ad.

followUps.redspher_cicd_failure_followup: Mikor lehet biztonságosabb a roll-forward, mint a rollback?

questions.title: Choosing a practical test strategy
questions.prompt: How would you divide tests between frontend units, backend units, API contracts, integrations and end-to-end flows for this role?
questions.answerStructureHint: Risk-based layers, contract protection, small E2E set.

expectedPoints.label: Uses fast tests for local logic
expectedPoints.description: Unit or component tests cover business rules, transformations and UI behavior with quick feedback.

expectedPoints.label: Protects service boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Contract and integration tests verify schemas, database behavior and important external adapters.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps end-to-end tests focused
expectedPoints.description: A small reliable set covers critical user journeys instead of duplicating every edge case through the UI.

followUps.prompt: Where would you test a carrier API timeout and retry policy?

hu.title: Praktikus test strategy választása
hu.prompt: Hogyan osztanád el a teszteket frontend unit, backend unit, API contract, integration és end-to-end flow között ennél a szerepnél?
hu.answerStructureHint: Risk-based layerek, contract protection, kevés E2E.

test_units.label: Gyors tesztet használ local logichoz
test_units.description: Unit vagy component test fedi a business rule-t, transzformációt és UI behaviort gyors feedbackkel.

test_contract.label: Védi a service boundaryket
test_contract.description: Contract és integration test ellenőrzi a schemát, database behaviort és fontos external adaptert.

test_e2e.label: Fókuszáltan tartja az end-to-end testeket
test_e2e.description: Kis, reliable készlet fedi a critical user journey-ket, nem minden edge case-et duplikál UI-n át.

followUps.redspher_cicd_test_strategy_followup: Hol tesztelnéd a carrier API timeoutot és retry policyt?

questions.title: Modular monolith versus microservices
questions.prompt: A small business-oriented team is starting a new logistics capability. How would you decide between a modular monolith and microservices?
questions.answerStructureHint: Team and domain boundaries, operational cost, extraction signals.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from context rather than pattern
expectedPoints.description: Considers team ownership, change rate, domain boundaries, scale and isolation needs.

expectedPoints.label: Makes distribution costs explicit
expectedPoints.description: Microservices add deployment, networking, observability, data consistency and operational complexity.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps an extraction path
expectedPoints.description: Uses internal module boundaries and extracts only when independent ownership, scaling or release needs become real.

followUps.prompt: What evidence would tell you that one module should become a service?

hu.title: Modular monolith vagy microservices
hu.prompt: Egy kis, business-oriented csapat új logistics capabilityt indít. Hogyan döntenél modular monolith és microservices között?
hu.answerStructureHint: Team és domain boundary, operational cost, extraction signal.

arch_context.label: Contextből indul, nem patternből
arch_context.description: Team ownershipöt, change rate-et, domain boundaryt, scale-t és isolation needet mérlegel.

arch_cost.label: Explicitté teszi a distribution costot
arch_cost.description: Microservice deployment, networking, observability, data consistency és operational complexity költséget ad.

arch_path.label: Extraction pathot hagy
arch_path.description: Internal module boundaryt használ, és csak valós independent ownership, scaling vagy release need esetén extractál.

followUps.redspher_arch_monolith_followup: Milyen evidence mutatná, hogy egy modult service-szé kell választani?

questions.title: Explaining an architecture decision
questions.prompt: Describe how you would present a technical decision to product and engineering stakeholders when no option is clearly best.
questions.answerStructureHint: Problem and criteria, options, reversible decision.

expectedPoints.label: Frames the decision around an outcome
expectedPoints.description: Explains the business goal, constraints and quality attributes in plain language.

expectedPoints.label: Compares realistic alternatives
expectedPoints.description: Shows two or three options including cost, risk, delivery speed and operational consequence.

expectedPoints.label: Records assumptions and revisit signals
expectedPoints.description: States the recommendation, what makes it reversible and which evidence triggers review.

followUps.prompt: How would you handle a stakeholder who prefers the most advanced option for prestige?

hu.title: Architecture döntés bemutatása
hu.prompt: Mutasd be, hogyan prezentálnál technikai döntést product és engineering stakeholder-eknek, amikor nincs egyértelműen legjobb opció.
hu.answerStructureHint: Probléma és criteria, opciók, reversible döntés.

arch_goal.label: Outcome köré keretezi a döntést
arch_goal.description: Business célt, constraintet és quality attribute-ot plain language-ben mond el.

arch_options.label: Reális alternatívákat hasonlít
arch_options.description: Két-három opciót mutat cost, risk, delivery speed és operational consequence mellett.

arch_revisit.label: Rögzíti az assumptiont és revisit signalt
arch_revisit.description: Kimondja a recommendationt, reversibilityt és azt az evidence-et, amely review-t indít.

followUps.redspher_arch_decision_followup: Hogyan kezelnél egy stakeholdert, aki presztízsből a legfejlettebb opciót preferálja?

questions.title: Avoiding premature scalability work
questions.prompt: How do you design for scalability without overengineering a system whose future load is uncertain?
questions.answerStructureHint: Known constraints, cheap options, measurement triggers.

expectedPoints.label: Separates known demand from guesses
expectedPoints.description: Uses current load, credible growth scenarios and explicit service objectives instead of vague internet scale.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses scalable basics with low complexity
expectedPoints.description: Keeps services stateless where useful, uses indexes and bounded work, and avoids unnecessary distribution.

expectedPoints.label: Defines measurable evolution triggers
expectedPoints.description: Load tests and production metrics define when to add caching, partitioning or independent scaling.

followUps.prompt: Give an example of a design choice that is cheap now but preserves a later scaling path.

hu.title: Korai scalability munka elkerülése
hu.prompt: Hogyan tervezel scalabilityre overengineering nélkül, ha a rendszer jövőbeli terhelése bizonytalan?
hu.answerStructureHint: Ismert constraint, olcsó opciók, measurement trigger.

arch_known.label: Elválasztja az ismert demandet a találgatástól
arch_known.description: Current loadot, hiteles growth scenariót és explicit service objective-et használ vague internet scale helyett.

arch_simple.label: Alacsony complexity mellett választ scalable alapokat
arch_simple.description: Hasznos esetben stateless service-t, indexet és bounded worköt használ, unnecessary distribution nélkül.

arch_trigger.label: Mérhető evolution triggert definiál
arch_trigger.description: Load test és production metric mondja meg, mikor kell caching, partitioning vagy independent scaling.

followUps.redspher_arch_scale_followup: Mondj példát olyan olcsó mai design döntésre, amely megőrzi a későbbi scaling pathot.

questions.title: Clarifying an ambiguous business request
questions.prompt: A stakeholder says: “We need faster pricing.” What questions would you ask before proposing a technical solution?
questions.answerStructureHint: Outcome, users and flow, measurable constraint.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies what faster means
expectedPoints.description: Distinguishes UI response time, end-to-end processing time, throughput and time to business decision.

expectedPoints.label: Walks through actors and exceptions
expectedPoints.description: Asks who uses it, current steps, data sources, cut-offs, manual work and important exceptional cases.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a measurable target
expectedPoints.description: Requests a baseline, target percentile or throughput and the business metric expected to improve.

followUps.prompt: What small experiment could distinguish a slow UI from a slow pricing process?

hu.title: Kétértelmű business request tisztázása
hu.prompt: Egy stakeholder azt mondja: „Gyorsabb pricing kell.” Milyen kérdéseket tennél fel technikai solution javaslata előtt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Outcome, user és flow, mérhető constraint.

business_meaning.label: Tisztázza, mit jelent a gyorsabb
business_meaning.description: Elválasztja a UI response time-ot, end-to-end processingt, throughputot és business decision idejét.

business_context.label: Végigjárja az actort és exceptionöket
business_context.description: Rákérdez a userre, jelenlegi step-ekre, data source-ra, cut-offra, manual workre és fontos exceptionre.

business_measure.label: Mérhető targetet definiál
business_measure.description: Baseline-t, target percentile-t vagy throughputot és javítandó business metricet kér.

followUps.redspher_business_ambiguity_followup: Milyen kis experiment különböztetné meg a lassú UI-t a lassú pricing processtől?

questions.title: Finding logistics edge cases
questions.prompt: How would you discover edge cases for a shipment booking or pricing workflow without trying to predict every possible exception alone?
questions.answerStructureHint: Concrete examples, domain collaboration, prioritization.

expectedPoints.label: Uses concrete scenario walkthroughs
expectedPoints.description: Walks through real recent cases, including delayed, missing, duplicate and conflicting information.

expectedPoints.label: Collaborates with domain and operations people
expectedPoints.description: Involves users, support and operational experts and inspects incident or exception history.

expectedPoints.label: Prioritizes by risk and frequency
expectedPoints.description: Turns important cases into acceptance criteria and defers extremely rare low-impact behavior deliberately.

followUps.prompt: How would you document an exception that requires manual intervention?

hu.title: Logistics edge case-ek feltárása
hu.prompt: Hogyan tárnád fel egy shipment booking vagy pricing workflow edge case-eit anélkül, hogy egyedül próbálnál minden exceptiont kitalálni?
hu.answerStructureHint: Konkrét példák, domain együttműködés, prioritization.

business_examples.label: Konkrét scenariókat jár végig
business_examples.description: Valós friss eseteket jár végig delayed, missing, duplicate és conflicting információval.

business_experts.label: Domain és operations szereplőkkel dolgozik
business_experts.description: Usert, supportot és operational expertet von be, valamint incident vagy exception historyt vizsgál.

business_priority.label: Risk és frequency alapján priorizál
business_priority.description: Fontos esetből acceptance criteriont készít, extrém ritka low-impact behaviort pedig tudatosan halaszt.

followUps.redspher_business_edge_cases_followup: Hogyan dokumentálnál egy manual interventiont igénylő exceptiont?

questions.title: Pushing back pragmatically
questions.prompt: A requested solution appears expensive and does not address the stated business outcome. How would you challenge it without blocking progress?
questions.answerStructureHint: Shared goal, evidence, alternative and experiment.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from the shared outcome
expectedPoints.description: Confirms the desired result and constraints instead of attacking the stakeholder suggestion.

expectedPoints.label: Explains cost and risk concretely
expectedPoints.description: Uses delivery effort, operational burden, dependency or data evidence rather than vague technical preference.

expectedPoints.label: Offers a smaller testable alternative
expectedPoints.description: Proposes a thin solution or experiment that tests the assumption and preserves momentum.

followUps.prompt: When would you accept the more expensive option despite your concerns?

hu.title: Pragmatikus szakmai visszajelzés
hu.prompt: Egy kért solution drágának tűnik és nem kezeli a megfogalmazott business outcome-ot. Hogyan kérdőjeleznéd meg úgy, hogy ne blokkold a progresst?
hu.answerStructureHint: Közös cél, evidence, alternatíva és experiment.

business_align.label: Közös outcome-ból indul
business_align.description: Megerősíti a kívánt eredményt és constraintet a stakeholder javaslat támadása helyett.

business_evidence.label: Konkrétan mutatja a costot és risket
business_evidence.description: Delivery effortet, operational burdent, dependencyt vagy data evidence-et használ vague preference helyett.

business_alternative.label: Kisebb tesztelhető alternatívát ad
business_alternative.description: Thin solutiont vagy experimentet javasol, amely teszteli az assumptiont és fenntartja a momentumot.

followUps.redspher_business_pushback_followup: Mikor fogadnád el a drágább opciót az aggályaid ellenére?

questions.title: Evaluating an LLM use case
questions.prompt: How would you decide whether a logistics workflow should use an LLM, deterministic code, or a combination of both?
questions.answerStructureHint: Task characteristics, consequence, hybrid boundary.

expectedPoints.label: Matches technology to the task
expectedPoints.description: LLMs fit ambiguous language and classification; exact rules, calculations and invariants belong in deterministic code.

expectedPoints.label: Considers the cost of a wrong result
expectedPoints.description: Uses error tolerance, reversibility, privacy, latency and human review to decide acceptable autonomy.

expectedPoints.label: Builds a controlled hybrid flow
expectedPoints.description: Lets the model interpret or propose while deterministic code validates, calculates and commits consequential changes.

followUps.prompt: Give one logistics task where you would deliberately avoid an LLM.

hu.title: LLM use case értékelése
hu.prompt: Hogyan döntenéd el, hogy egy logistics workflow LLM-et, deterministic kódot vagy ezek kombinációját használja?
hu.answerStructureHint: Task jellemzői, consequence, hybrid boundary.

llm_task.label: A technológiát a taskhoz illeszti
llm_task.description: LLM ambiguous language-hez és classificationhöz jó; exact rule, calculation és invariant deterministic kódba való.

llm_risk.label: Mérlegeli a rossz eredmény költségét
llm_risk.description: Error tolerance, reversibility, privacy, latency és human review alapján dönt az elfogadható autonomy-ról.

llm_hybrid.label: Controlled hybrid flow-t épít
llm_hybrid.description: A model értelmez vagy javasol, deterministic kód validál, számol és commitol consequential change-et.

followUps.redspher_llm_use_case_followup: Mondj egy logistics taskot, ahol tudatosan kerülnéd az LLM-et.

questions.title: Grounding an internal AI assistant
questions.prompt: An assistant must answer questions about current pricing rules and shipments. How would you supply trustworthy context and reduce hallucination?
questions.answerStructureHint: Authoritative retrieval, scoped context, uncertainty.

expectedPoints.label: Retrieves from authoritative sources
expectedPoints.description: Uses current approved rules and authorized shipment data rather than relying on model memory.

expectedPoints.label: Limits and structures context
expectedPoints.description: Filters by user permission and task relevance, adds source identifiers and controls context size.

expectedPoints.label: Handles missing evidence explicitly
expectedPoints.description: Requires the assistant to state when evidence is missing, avoid unsupported claims and route high-risk cases to a person.

followUps.prompt: How would you evaluate whether retrieval improved answer quality?

hu.title: Internal AI assistant groundingja
hu.prompt: Egy assistantnek aktuális pricing rule-okról és shipmentekről kell válaszolnia. Hogyan adnál trusted contextet és csökkentenéd a hallucinationt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Authoritative retrieval, scoped context, uncertainty.

llm_sources.label: Authoritative source-ból retrieve-ol
llm_sources.description: Aktuális approved rule-t és authorized shipment adatot használ model memory helyett.

llm_scope.label: Korlátozza és strukturálja a contextet
llm_scope.description: User permission és task relevance alapján szűr, source id-t ad és kontrollálja a context size-t.

llm_uncertainty.label: Expliciten kezeli a missing evidence-et
llm_uncertainty.description: A válasz jelzi a hiányzó evidence-et, kerüli az unsupported claimet és high-risk esetet emberhez küld.

followUps.redspher_llm_grounding_followup: Hogyan értékelnéd, hogy a retrieval javította-e az answer qualityt?

questions.title: Evaluating an LLM feature before release
questions.prompt: What would an evaluation plan look like for an AI feature that explains unusual shipment prices?
questions.answerStructureHint: Dataset and criteria, baselines, continuous evaluation.

expectedPoints.label: Builds a representative evaluation set
expectedPoints.description: Includes common, difficult, missing-data and unsafe cases reviewed by domain experts.

expectedPoints.label: Defines task-specific quality and operational metrics
expectedPoints.description: Measures factual grounding, rule coverage, harmful errors, latency and cost rather than one generic score.

expectedPoints.label: Compares versions and monitors production
expectedPoints.description: Uses a deterministic or human baseline, regression gates and sampled production review with feedback.

followUps.prompt: Why is user thumbs-up feedback insufficient as the only quality metric?

hu.title: LLM feature értékelése release előtt
hu.prompt: Hogyan nézne ki egy evaluation plan egy olyan AI feature-höz, amely szokatlan shipment price-okat magyaráz?
hu.answerStructureHint: Dataset és criteria, baseline, folyamatos evaluation.

llm_eval_set.label: Reprezentatív evaluation setet épít
llm_eval_set.description: Common, difficult, missing-data és unsafe case-eket tartalmaz domain expert review-val.

llm_eval_metrics.label: Task-specific quality és operational metricet definiál
llm_eval_metrics.description: Factual groundingot, rule coverage-et, harmful errort, latencyt és costot mér egy generic score helyett.

llm_eval_loop.label: Verziót hasonlít és productiont monitoroz
llm_eval_loop.description: Deterministic vagy human baseline-t, regression gate-et és sampled production review-t használ feedbackkel.

followUps.redspher_llm_evaluation_followup: Miért nem elég a user thumbs-up feedback egyetlen quality metricnek?

questions.title: Explaining MCP in an architecture interview
questions.prompt: What is the Model Context Protocol, which components participate, and what problem does it solve?
questions.answerStructureHint: Protocol purpose, components, control boundary.

expectedPoints.label: Defines the interoperability purpose
expectedPoints.description: MCP standardizes how AI applications discover and use external tools, resources or prompts.

expectedPoints.label: Names host or client and server roles
expectedPoints.description: The host or client manages model interaction; the server exposes capabilities with schemas.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps execution under application control
expectedPoints.description: The model can request a call, but the host validates permission and executes it.

followUps.prompt: How is MCP different from simply calling a REST API from application code?

hu.title: MCP elmagyarázása architecture interjún
hu.prompt: Mi a Model Context Protocol, mely komponensek vesznek részt benne, és milyen problémát old meg?
hu.answerStructureHint: Protocol cél, komponensek, control boundary.

mcp_purpose.label: Definiálja az interoperability célt
mcp_purpose.description: Az MCP szabványosítja, hogyan fedez fel és használ egy AI app external toolt, resource-t vagy promptot.

mcp_components.label: Megnevezi a host/client és server szerepet
mcp_components.description: A host vagy client kezeli a model interactiont; a server schemával tesz elérhetővé capabilityket.

mcp_control.label: Application control alatt tartja az executiont
mcp_control.description: A model kérheti a callt, de a host validál permissiont és végrehajt.

followUps.redspher_mcp_explain_followup: Miben különbözik az MCP attól, hogy application code-ból REST API-t hívunk?

questions.title: Designing a safe MCP tool
questions.prompt: Design an MCP tool that lets an operations assistant propose a shipment repricing. What should its schema and execution boundary contain?
questions.answerStructureHint: Narrow schema, authorization and validation, safe result.

expectedPoints.label: Defines a narrow explicit tool contract
expectedPoints.description: Uses a specific action name, shipment id, proposed value, reason and constrained types instead of arbitrary commands.

expectedPoints.label: Enforces identity and business authorization
expectedPoints.description: The backend checks the current user, shipment access and allowed pricing rules independently of model text.

expectedPoints.label: Returns bounded auditable output
expectedPoints.description: Returns a proposal or operation id with safe errors, records the invocation and requires confirmation for the write.

followUps.prompt: Would you expose one generic update_shipment tool or several narrow tools? Why?

hu.title: Biztonságos MCP tool tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezz MCP toolt, amelyben egy operations assistant shipment repricingot javasol. Mit tartalmazzon a schema és execution boundary?
hu.answerStructureHint: Szűk schema, authorization és validation, safe result.

mcp_schema.label: Szűk explicit tool contractot definiál
mcp_schema.description: Specific action nevet, shipment id-t, proposed value-t, reasont és constrained típust használ arbitrary command helyett.

mcp_auth.label: Identityt és business authorizationt enforce-ol
mcp_auth.description: A backend a model texttől függetlenül ellenőrzi a usert, shipment accesst és pricing rule-t.

mcp_result.label: Bounded és auditable outputot ad
mcp_result.description: Proposal vagy operation id jön vissza safe errorral, audit log készül, write előtt confirmation kell.

followUps.redspher_mcp_tool_followup: Egy generic update_shipment toolt vagy több szűk toolt tennél elérhetővé? Miért?

questions.title: Controlling MCP resource access
questions.prompt: An MCP server can expose shipment documents and pricing rules. How would you prevent excessive context, cross-customer access and stale information?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identity-scoped retrieval, bounded result, freshness.

expectedPoints.label: Scopes every request to the authenticated identity
expectedPoints.description: Authorization filters customer, role and resource ownership before content is returned.

expectedPoints.label: Returns only relevant bounded content
expectedPoints.description: Uses query parameters, pagination, summaries and size limits instead of dumping entire repositories.

expectedPoints.label: Makes freshness and source visible
expectedPoints.description: Provides timestamps or versions, retrieves from authoritative stores and avoids caching sensitive stale data blindly.

followUps.prompt: What information about a resource should be included so the model can cite it reliably?

hu.title: MCP resource access kontrollálása
hu.prompt: Egy MCP server shipment dokumentumokat és pricing rule-okat tehet elérhetővé. Hogyan előznéd meg a túl sok contextet, cross-customer accesst és stale információt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Identity-scoped retrieval, bounded result, freshness.

mcp_scope.label: Authenticated identityre szűkít minden requestet
mcp_scope.description: Authorization customer, role és resource ownership alapján szűr content visszaadása előtt.

mcp_bound.label: Csak releváns bounded contentet ad
mcp_bound.description: Query parametert, paginationt, summaryt és size limitet használ entire repository dump helyett.

mcp_fresh.label: Láthatóvá teszi a freshness-t és source-t
mcp_fresh.description: Timestampot vagy versiont ad, authoritative store-ból retrieve-ol, és nem cache-el vakon sensitive stale datát.

followUps.redspher_mcp_resource_followup: Milyen információt kell a resource-hoz adni, hogy a model megbízhatóan hivatkozhasson rá?

questions.title: Designing a bounded agent loop
questions.prompt: Design the control loop for an agent that investigates shipment exceptions using several tools. How do you stop it from running indefinitely?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explicit state, bounded actions, stop and recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Represents workflow state explicitly
expectedPoints.description: Stores the goal, observations, completed actions and remaining decision outside the model prompt alone.

expectedPoints.label: Applies hard execution limits
expectedPoints.description: Uses maximum steps, elapsed time, token or cost budgets and per-tool rate limits.

expectedPoints.label: Defines outcomes and recovery
expectedPoints.description: Stops on success, lack of progress, policy violation or uncertainty, and can resume or escalate with an audit trail.

followUps.prompt: How would you detect that the agent is repeating semantically equivalent actions?

hu.title: Bounded agent loop tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezd meg egy több toolt használó shipment exceptiont vizsgáló agent control loopját. Hogyan akadályozod meg, hogy végtelenül fusson?
hu.answerStructureHint: Explicit state, bounded action, stop és recovery.

agent_state.label: Expliciten reprezentálja a workflow state-et
agent_state.description: A goal, observation, completed action és remaining decision nem csak a model promptban van tárolva.

agent_limits.label: Hard execution limitet alkalmaz
agent_limits.description: Maximum stepet, elapsed time-ot, token vagy cost budgetet és per-tool rate limitet használ.

agent_stop.label: Outcome-ot és recoveryt definiál
agent_stop.description: Success, lack of progress, policy violation vagy uncertainty esetén megáll, audit trail mellett resume-olható vagy escalate-elhető.

followUps.redspher_agent_loop_followup: Hogyan észlelnéd, hogy az agent szemantikailag ugyanazt az actiont ismétli?

questions.title: Persisting a long-running agent workflow
questions.prompt: An agent workflow can wait hours for carrier data or human approval. What state would you persist, and how would it resume safely after a restart?
questions.answerStructureHint: Durable state machine, idempotent steps, event correlation.

expectedPoints.label: Persists an explicit durable state machine
expectedPoints.description: Stores workflow id, current state, inputs, tool results, approvals, timestamps and version.

expectedPoints.label: Makes steps idempotent and transactional
expectedPoints.description: Each action has a unique operation key and records completion before retrying or advancing.

expectedPoints.label: Correlates delayed events safely
expectedPoints.description: External results and approvals include workflow and expected-state identifiers so stale events cannot advance the wrong run.

followUps.prompt: How would you migrate active workflows after changing the state-machine definition?

hu.title: Hosszú agent workflow state-jének tárolása
hu.prompt: Egy agent workflow órákig várhat carrier adatra vagy human approvalra. Milyen state-et tárolnál, és hogyan folytatná biztonságosan restart után?
hu.answerStructureHint: Durable state machine, idempotens step, event correlation.

agent_durable.label: Explicit durable state machine-t tárol
agent_durable.description: Workflow id-t, current state-et, inputot, tool resultot, approvalt, timestampot és versiont tárol.

agent_idempotent.label: Idempotenssé és transactionallá teszi a stepeket
agent_idempotent.description: Minden action unique operation key-t kap, completiont rögzít retry vagy továbblépés előtt.

agent_events.label: Biztonságosan korrelálja a delayed eventeket
agent_events.description: External result és approval workflow- és expected-state id-t tartalmaz, így stale event nem léptet rossz run-t.

followUps.redspher_agent_state_followup: Hogyan migrálnád az aktív workflow-kat a state-machine definíció módosítása után?

questions.title: One agent versus multiple agents
questions.prompt: When would you use multiple specialized agents instead of one agent with several tools, and what new problems would that introduce?
questions.answerStructureHint: Justified specialization, coordination cost, measurable choice.

expectedPoints.label: Requires a real specialization benefit
expectedPoints.description: Multiple agents may help when tasks need distinct context, policies, models or independent ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Makes coordination risks explicit
expectedPoints.description: Adds message passing, duplicated context, conflicting decisions, latency, cost and harder debugging.

expectedPoints.label: Starts with the simpler measurable baseline
expectedPoints.description: Prefers one orchestrated agent or deterministic workflow until evaluation shows specialization improves outcomes.

followUps.prompt: How would you trace responsibility when two agents disagree?

hu.title: Egy agent vagy több agent
hu.prompt: Mikor használnál több specialized agentet egy több toolos agent helyett, és milyen új problémákat hozna ez?
hu.answerStructureHint: Indokolt specializáció, coordination cost, mérhető döntés.

agent_specialize.label: Valódi specialization benefitet követel
agent_specialize.description: Több agent akkor segíthet, ha distinct context, policy, model vagy independent ownership szükséges.

agent_coordination.label: Explicitté teszi a coordination risket
agent_coordination.description: Message passinget, duplicated contextet, conflicting decisiont, latencyt, costot és nehezebb debuggingot hoz.

agent_baseline.label: Egyszerű mérhető baseline-nal indul
agent_baseline.description: Egy orchestrated agentet vagy deterministic workflow-t preferál, amíg evaluation nem bizonyít javulást.

followUps.redspher_agent_multi_followup: Hogyan trace-elnéd a felelősséget, ha két agent nem ért egyet?

questions.title: Defending against prompt injection
questions.prompt: An internal assistant reads carrier emails that may contain instructions aimed at the model. How would you reduce prompt-injection risk?
questions.answerStructureHint: Untrusted content, deterministic permissions, action confirmation.

expectedPoints.label: Treats retrieved content as untrusted data
expectedPoints.description: External email text is labelled and separated from system policy rather than obeyed as instruction.

expectedPoints.label: Enforces permissions outside the model
expectedPoints.description: Tool allowlists, schemas, user authorization and business rules are checked by application code.

expectedPoints.label: Adds confirmation and limits for consequential actions
expectedPoints.description: Write actions require explicit confirmation, scoped parameters, rate limits and audit logs.

followUps.prompt: Why is telling the model to ignore malicious instructions not sufficient?

hu.title: Prompt injection elleni védelem
hu.prompt: Egy internal assistant carrier emaileket olvas, amelyek a modelnek szánt instructiont is tartalmazhatnak. Hogyan csökkentenéd a prompt-injection risket?
hu.answerStructureHint: Untrusted content, deterministic permission, action confirmation.

guard_untrusted.label: Untrusted adatként kezeli a retrieved contentet
guard_untrusted.description: Az external email szöveg labelt kap és elkülönül a system policytől, nem instructionként hajtódik végre.

guard_enforce.label: A modellen kívül enforce-olja a permissiont
guard_enforce.description: Tool allowlistet, schemát, user authorizationt és business rule-t application code ellenőriz.

guard_consequence.label: Confirmationt és limitet ad consequential actionhöz
guard_consequence.description: Write action explicit confirmationt, scoped parametert, rate limitet és audit logot igényel.

followUps.redspher_ai_injection_followup: Miért nem elég azt mondani a modelnek, hogy hagyja figyelmen kívül a malicious instructiont?

questions.title: Protecting sensitive data in an AI workflow
questions.prompt: How would you design an AI feature that uses customer shipment data while minimizing privacy and data-leakage risk?
questions.answerStructureHint: Data minimization, provider boundary, retention and access.

expectedPoints.label: Minimizes and classifies data
expectedPoints.description: Sends only fields required for the task, removes secrets or identifiers where possible and classifies sensitivity.

expectedPoints.label: Controls the model-provider boundary
expectedPoints.description: Reviews region, training use, encryption, contractual controls and approved model endpoints.

expectedPoints.label: Restricts storage, logs and user access
expectedPoints.description: Uses short retention, redacted telemetry, tenant authorization and auditable access.

followUps.prompt: Which information would you avoid placing in model prompts even for an internal system?

hu.title: Sensitive data védelme AI workflow-ban
hu.prompt: Hogyan terveznél customer shipment adatot használó AI feature-t privacy és data-leakage risk minimalizálásával?
hu.answerStructureHint: Data minimization, provider boundary, retention és access.

guard_minimize.label: Minimalizálja és osztályozza az adatot
guard_minimize.description: Csak taskhoz szükséges fieldet küld, lehetőleg eltávolít secretet vagy identifiert, és sensitivityt osztályoz.

guard_provider.label: Kontrollálja a model-provider boundaryt
guard_provider.description: Regiont, training use-t, encryptiont, contractual controlt és approved model endpointot vizsgál.

guard_retention.label: Korlátozza a storage-ot, logot és user accesst
guard_retention.description: Short retentiont, redacted telemetryt, tenant authorizationt és auditable accesst használ.

followUps.redspher_ai_data_followup: Milyen információt kerülnél a model promptban még internal rendszer esetén is?

questions.title: Observing AI behavior in production
questions.prompt: What would you log and monitor for an agent-based workflow without creating a new sensitive-data problem?
questions.answerStructureHint: Traceability, quality and safety metrics, privacy.

expectedPoints.label: Creates a structured execution trace
expectedPoints.description: Records workflow id, model and prompt version, tool names, decisions, latency, token use and outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Monitors task and safety outcomes
expectedPoints.description: Tracks success, escalation, retry loops, policy blocks, incorrect actions, latency and cost.

expectedPoints.label: Redacts and controls observability data
expectedPoints.description: Stores hashes, identifiers or sampled redacted content, with access and retention controls.

followUps.prompt: How would you investigate a harmful tool call while preserving user privacy?

hu.title: AI viselkedés observabilityje productionben
hu.prompt: Mit logolnál és monitoroznál egy agent-based workflow-nál anélkül, hogy új sensitive-data problémát okoznál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Traceability, quality és safety metric, privacy.

guard_trace.label: Structured execution trace-t készít
guard_trace.description: Workflow id-t, model és prompt versiont, tool nevet, decisiont, latencyt, token use-t és outcome-ot rögzít.

guard_metrics.label: Task és safety outcome-ot monitoroz
guard_metrics.description: Success, escalation, retry loop, policy block, incorrect action, latency és cost metricet követ.

guard_privacy.label: Redactálja és kontrollálja az observability adatot
guard_privacy.description: Hash, identifier vagy sampled redacted content kerül tárolásra access és retention control mellett.

followUps.redspher_ai_observe_followup: Hogyan vizsgálnál ki harmful tool callt a user privacy megőrzése mellett?

questions.title: First steps with a legacy PHP integration
questions.prompt: You must add a TypeScript feature that depends on an undocumented PHP pricing module. What would you do before changing or replacing it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Map behavior, protect with tests, create a seam.

expectedPoints.label: Maps behavior and consumers
expectedPoints.description: Reviews callers, data flow, production examples, logs and domain experts to find hidden rules.

expectedPoints.label: Adds characterization and contract tests
expectedPoints.description: Captures current inputs and outputs, especially important edge cases, before refactoring.
